title: docs: link app submission criteria from tier-app submission template
author: TRDEFI
pr_body_excerpt: Closes #11898 ## Description Adds a link from the Requirements section of the app submission template to the evaluation criteria in `apps/README.md` so submitters know what reviewers look for. Also adds a stable HTML anchor (``) so the link target doesn't break if the heading is renamed. ## Changes 1. **`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/tier-app-submission.yml`** — added: `- Read the [submission criteria](apps/README.md#what-makes-a-good-app-submission) before submitting` 2. **`apps/README.md`** — added `` anchor before the "What Makes a Good App Submission" section

gist.headline: Mapping the garden path
gist.blurb: We've added a handy link to the app submission criteria right in the GitHub issue template. Now you know exactly what we're looking for before you hit submit!
gist.summary: Added a direct link to the app evaluation criteria in the tier-app submission issue template, along with a stable HTML anchor in the apps README.
gist.impact: Contributors can now easily review the 'What Makes a Good App Submission' guidelines directly from the GitHub issue template before submitting their apps.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee mascot reading a map or a checklist next to a glowing retro terminal, representing clear guidelines and documentation.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing tiny reading glasses, holding a small pixelated checklist next to a glowing retro terminal. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
